title: 10 best herbal tinctures for pimples

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins in sage work to reduce the swelling and redness associated with acne, while its antioxidant compounds help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to breakouts. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that can contribute to clogged pores and acne formation, making it an effective natural remedy for clearing up pimples.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sage tincture's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and thujone, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin that can cause acne and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples, promoting a faster healing process.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to premature aging, acne, and other skin issues.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can lead to pimples.

Salvianolic acid: A type of phenolic acid, salvianolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation that can cause pimples.

Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound, rosmarinic acid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation that can lead to pimples, as well as promote wound healing and skin health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with pimples.
Flowers: The flowers have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples.
Stems: The stems contain antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the skin, making them effective in treating pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50-100ml of high-proof alcohol such as ethanol or methanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the plant material in the solvent for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, work together to reduce the appearance of acne by killing bacteria that cause breakouts and soothing redness and swelling. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for pimples. By using herbal lemon balm tinctures topically or internally, individuals can achieve clear and healthy-looking skin with fewer imperfections.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antibacterial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to combat P. acnes bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further acne breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon balm tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pimples, promoting a faster healing process.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of acne by protecting the skin from oxidative stress.
Melissic acid: A phenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, which can help control bacterial growth on the skin, a common cause of acne.
Limonene: A terpene that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of acne by protecting the skin from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in pimples.
Flowers: They are used because of their antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the occurrence of pimples.
Stems: They are used due to their astringent properties, which can help reduce oil production and tighten pores, making them less prone to pimples.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of melissa officinalis flowers and 500ml of vodka in a clean dark glass jar.
Steep the melissa officinalis flowers in the vodka for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in the fridge to slow down fermentation and spoilage.
After 2-3 months allow the tincture to mature and then use it as needed to support health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also combating bacterial infections that can cause breakouts. Additionally, the antiseptic qualities of pot marigold help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora. As a result, this herbal tincture has been traditionally used to effectively alleviate the appearance of pimples and other acne-related issues.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Pot marigold tincture's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in pot marigold tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of acne scars.

Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to oxidative stress, which is believed to be a factor in the development of acne, thereby protecting the skin from harm.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the severity and frequency of pimples by reducing redness and fighting bacterial infections.

Carotenoids (specifically, Calendula's carotenoid derivative, Calendic acid): These pigments possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and promoting skin health.

Phenolic acids (specifically, Calendula's phenolic acid, Calendulin): These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation associated with pimples, promoting a healthy and clear complexion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which help soothe and heal pimples.
Leaves: Leaves of Calendula officinalis contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating pimples.

Stems: Stems of Calendula officinalis may also be used, as they contain some of the same anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds found in the flowers and leaves, which can aid in pimple treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of calendula officinalis when fully open and dry them thoroughly to prevent mold.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with pimples because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties. When applied topically or taken internally as a supplement, aloe vera helps to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne-prone skin. Its antimicrobial agents also work to eliminate bacteria that can cause breakouts, while its moisturizing properties calm and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and promoting healthy skin regeneration. As a result, aloe tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for pimple-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, making it an effective remedy for soothing inflamed pimples.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera, such as aloin and acemannan, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster healing.

Wound-healing and tissue-regenerating: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping to repair damaged skin and reduce scarring from acne lesions.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone glycoside that helps with pimples by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which reduce redness and prevent bacterial infections that can cause acne.

Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides: Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that help with pimples by stimulating the production of hyaluronic acid, which retains moisture and soothes the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.

Lupeol: Lupeol is a triterpene that helps with pimples by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and protect the skin from oxidative stress, thereby preventing the formation of acne lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part for treating pimples due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

Roots: Aloe vera roots are also used to treat pimples due to their content of aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, as well as its ability to reduce sebum production.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds are sometimes used in tinctures for pimples due to their content of fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that can help to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and wash them thoroughly with cool water.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Transfer the herbal tincture to glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with pimples because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling. The tannins present in dandelion roots also have a natural astringent effect, which can help to dry out acne-causing bacteria and reduce sebum production, thus preventing clogged pores. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate digestion and promote detoxification can help to eliminate toxins from the body that may contribute to acne. This makes dandelion tinctures a natural and effective remedy for combating pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in skin tissue, helping to alleviate redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Antibacterial properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion tincture have been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne and pimples, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to pimple formation, while also protecting against environmental stressors and pollution.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: It helps to reduce inflammation and prevent acne formation by inhibiting the expression of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Polysaccharides: They have antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is commonly associated with acne.
Fatty acids (specifically, linoleic acid): They help to regulate the skin's oil production, prevent clogged pores, and reduce inflammation, thereby preventing the formation of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the spring or fall when the plant is not in flower.
Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly to remove dirt and debris with distilled water.
Combine 1 part taraxacum officinale plant material with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the dark for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium tinctures helps with pimples because it is a natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agent that targets the root cause of acne. The tincture's potent compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, work to reduce redness and swelling by calming irritated skin, while also combating bacteria that can clog pores and lead to breakouts. Additionally, wild geranium's antiseptic properties help to purify the skin and promote a healthy balance of skin flora, resulting in clearer, more radiant skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Wild geranium tincture has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which can contribute to the formation of acne and pimples.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples, promoting a more even skin tone and reduced inflammation.

Antioxidant property: Wild geranium tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and skin aging, and may also help reduce the appearance of acne scars and hyperpigmentation.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Geranium maculatum tinctures may help with pimples by reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of infection, thereby promoting a faster recovery of the affected skin.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, ellagic acid): Phenolic acids in Geranium maculatum tinctures may help with pimples by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infections that often lead to acne and pimples.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): Flavonoids in Geranium maculatum tinctures may help with pimples by possessing antioxidant properties that can reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and free radical damage, all of which can contribute to the development of pimples and acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and reduce acne.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures due to their astringent and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in pimples.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and promote healing in acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh geranium maculatum roots and stems harvested in the spring.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent penetration.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The tincture's antibacterial compounds also target P. acnes bacteria, the primary cause of pimples, preventing them from multiplying and spreading. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and hyperpigmentation, leaving skin looking clearer and more even-toned. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing pimple-prone skin.

Antimicrobial activity: The tannins and phenolic compounds present in Kansas coneflower exhibit antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that can contribute to acne formation and inflammation.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples.

Caffeic acid derivatives: Caffeic acid derivatives have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, potentially helping to reduce pimple severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of echinacoside, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Buds: The buds of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high content of alkaloids, which may help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause pimples.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are also used for their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in late summer or early fall when roots are fully developed.
Clean and dry the roots to remove any dirt or debris using paper bags or a drying rack for 2 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 percent vodka or ethanol as a solvent in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to extract the medicinal compounds.

Barks: The barks of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make tinctures because they contain tannins and other compounds that help to dry out pimples, reduce inflammation, and prevent bacterial growth.

Roots: The roots of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing the occurrence of pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort tinctures helps with pimples because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St. John's Wort tincture help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with pimples, making it an effective natural remedy for calming irritated skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers on a dry sunny day for maximum potency.
Combine one part dried hypericum perforatum herb with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, to release bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining liquid gold.
Store the hypericum perforatum tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature, allowing it to mature for at least three months before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves at dawn when dew is present and store them in an airtight container.
Weigh 200g of dried or 400g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Combine chopped herbs with 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar to achieve a 1:5 herb-to-ethanol ratio and seal the jar tightly.
Place the jar in a dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily, allowing the mixture to steep and extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid tincture in a cool dark place for future use.

Antimicrobial property: Licorice extract contains antimicrobial compounds like phenolic acids, which can help combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, reducing the severity of acne breakouts and preventing new ones from forming.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and macerate them in 250 milliliters of vodka for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and squeeze out as much liquid as possible from the plant material.
Add the resulting tincture to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool dry place for 14 days.
After 14 days, filter the tincture again and mix it with distilled water to create a 1:2 glycerite, diluting the herbal extract to 50% strength.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon grass tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture contains compounds such as citral and geraniol, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, the tincture's antibacterial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that can cause pimples. When used topically or taken orally, lemon grass tinctures may help soothe and clear up pimples, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemongrass tincture has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that is commonly associated with acne, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tannins and flavonoids present in lemongrass have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by pimples, allowing the skin to heal faster.

Antioxidant property: Lemongrass tincture is rich in antioxidants, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of acne, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Rhyzomes: Contains oils with antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections causing acne, while also reducing sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon schoenanthus leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants and debris.
Cleanly wash the plant material with filtered water, then gently shake off excess moisture.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction, using 100g of plant material per 500ml of menstruum.
Combine the chopped plant material with high-proof ethanol or glycerin as the solvent, stirring well to ensure uniform mixing and storage in a dark glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate for at least 2 weeks, shaking the container daily to aid extraction, before straining and filtering the resulting tincture according to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tincture's active compounds, including beta-carotene and salicylic acid, work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne-prone skin. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich properties of damask rose help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of skin pH. By using herbal damask rose tinctures, individuals can effectively combat pimples and achieve clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Damask rose tincture's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) and fungi like Malassezia, reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of pimples.

Anti-Inflammatory and Reducing Prostaglandin Production: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples by inhibiting prostaglandin production, a hormone-like substance that can exacerbate acne.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Properties: Damask rose tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and contribute to skin inflammation and damage, thereby reducing the appearance of pimples and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Flowers: The flowers of the damask rose plant are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa centifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and aroma.
Cleanly chop the harvested flowers into small pieces to increase surface area exposure.
Combine one part of chopped roses with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily for three minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with pimples because of its powerful antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal remedy contains saponins, which have been shown to combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing redness and swelling associated with breakouts. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting a reduction in inflammation and excess oil production. By addressing these underlying factors, wild potato tinctures can provide effective natural relief from pimples and help achieve clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Wild potato tincture contains compounds like saponins and polyphenols that have antibacterial effects, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause acne and pimples.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples by reducing inflammation in the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Wild potato tincture contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of pimples. By combating oxidative stress, these antioxidants can help promote healthy skin and reduce acne symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections associated with pimples.
Rhyzomes: Containing starches and flavonoids, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum kok saghyz roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and pack them tightly into a clean glass jar leaving one inch of headspace.
Combine 100 ml of high-proof vodka and 20 g of chopped taraxacum kok saghyz roots in the jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The herb's active compounds, such as citral and limonene, work together to reduce the severity and frequency of acne by combating bacteria that cause breakouts. Lemon verbena also has astringent and toning properties, which help to balance skin pH and tighten pores, reducing their appearance and preventing new pimples from forming. By using lemon verbena tinctures, individuals can enjoy a clearer, healthier-looking complexion with reduced inflammation and acne severity.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon verbena's antibacterial compounds, such as citral and limonene, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin that can cause acne and pimples.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon verbena tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples and acne.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to skin cells, which can lead to the formation of pimples and acne scars.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Leaves: Lemon verbena leaves contain essential oils that exhibit antibacterial activity, helping to combat the bacteria that cause acne and pimple formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems on a sunny day at peak flowering.
Wash the harvested plant material with clean water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop the washed plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar.
Pour 60-80% vodka or grain alcohol over the packed plant material, making sure all parts are covered.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to allow steeping and maturation slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work together to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause acne. As a result, elder tincture can help to shrink the size of pimples, reduce redness and swelling, and prevent new breakouts from forming. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may also help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting a clearer complexion overall.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Elder tincture's antibacterial properties help combat acne-causing bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) and Staphylococcus aureus, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, promoting a faster healing process and minimizing the appearance of acne scars.

Antioxidant: Elder tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect skin cells from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne formation and premature aging, ultimately reducing the severity and frequency of pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the elder plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth in pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from the wild or cultivate them in your garden.
Chop the harvested herbs into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped herbs with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are completely submerged.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help to combat acne-causing bacteria that can lead to breakouts. The tannins present in fennel also have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. Additionally, the herb's natural antioxidants help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting healthy cell regeneration and collagen production. By incorporating fennel tinctures into one's skincare routine, individuals can experience a noticeable reduction in acne severity and improved overall skin clarity.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Fennel's antibacterial compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help combat bacterial growth on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further acne formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, like sesquiterpene lactones, reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, promoting faster healing and minimizing scarring.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne formation, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain oils that help to balance skin pH and reduce sebum production, making them useful in treating oily skin and acne-prone areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in their optimal freshness for the best quality extract.
Combine 200 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare plant material with 1000 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow steeping.
After the steeping period strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them accurately indicating preparation date and herbal contents.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for pimples are concentrated plant extracts used to treat acne and reduce inflammation. They work by regulating hormonal imbalances, soothing irritated skin, and fighting bacterial infections that cause pimples. Effective herbal tinctures for pimples include Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Silybum marianum, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, Saponaria officinalis, Hamamelis virginiana, and Taraxacum officinale. If left untreated, pimples can cause scarring, low self-esteem, and social anxiety, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pimples that are treated with herbal tinctures are acne, rosacea, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions cause inflammation, redness, and breakouts on the skin, which can be soothed and reduced with herbal remedies. Tinctures made from herbs like tea tree oil, lavender, chamomile, and calendula have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and soothing properties that help to calm and clear the skin. These tinctures can be used topically or taken internally to promote overall skin health and reduce symptoms of these common skin issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with pimples is a blend of Calendula, Tea Tree, and Echinacea. Calendula tincture soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Tea Tree tincture has antimicrobial properties that combat acne-causing bacteria. Echinacea tincture boosts the immune system and fights off infections. Combining these three tinctures can help to reduce pimple severity, prevent scarring, and promote healthy skin. Use 5-10 drops of each tincture in a carrier oil or water three times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture contains triterpenoid compounds, which soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples. Additionally, its antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause breakouts, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes). By addressing both inflammation and bacterial overgrowth, Calendula officinalis tinctures can help to promote a clear and healthy complexion, reducing the appearance and occurrence of pimples.
remedy_constituents: Isochlorogenic acid: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can contribute to the development and severity of pimples.
Nordamycin: Nordamycin has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which can cause pimples and acne.
Quercetin: Quercetin is an anti-inflammatory compound that reduces redness and swelling associated with pimples, while also helping to protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial and fungal infections, making them a suitable choice for treating pimples.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe and treat pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula flowers on a dry sunny morning after 9am to ensure potency.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 35c for 2 hours to prevent moisture retention.
Combine 100g of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of 35% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with pimples because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively target and eliminate acne-causing bacteria. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected skin. Additionally, its antiseptic properties prevent the spread of bacteria, reducing the risk of further breakouts. By tackling the root cause of pimples, Melaleuca alternifolia tincture supports a healthy and balanced skin microbiome, leading to clearer and smoother skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with pimples by reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria that cause acne.
Limonene: Aids in the reduction of acne by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause pimples and also by reducing inflammation.
Linalool: Contributes to acne reduction by its anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to reduce the production of sebum, a key factor in the development of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high levels of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce acne and soothe irritated skin.
Buds: They are rich in melaleuca oil, which has antiseptic and antifungal properties that aid in preventing the spread of bacteria that cause pimples and acne.
Stems: They contain a high concentration of melaleuca oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and combat the bacteria that cause pimples and acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melaleuca alternifolia leaves in early morning when dew is still present on the leaves for 3 hours.
Clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine 1 part of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a glass container for 2 weeks.
Shake the container daily for 2 weeks to facilitate the extraction of active compounds from the plant material.
Strain and filter the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. The aloe vera's ability to balance the skin's natural pH and hydrate the skin also helps to prevent clogged pores and reduce the occurrence of pimples. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause pimples, making it an effective natural remedy to prevent and treat acne. This leads to a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, thereby preventing the formation of pimples.
Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help to reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores, which can lead to pimples.
Fatty acids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, while also promoting skin hydration, which can help to heal existing pimples and prevent new ones from forming.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain gel and sap that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Roots: Aloe vera roots contain a compound called aloin, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves in the morning when they are most potent and fresh for tincture making.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours or longer.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine with 80 proof ethanol in a 1 to 4 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer to a glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The aloe vera plant's gel-like substance is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples. The tincture's ability to regulate the skin's pH balance and promote healthy skin cell growth also helps to prevent future breakouts, resulting in clearer and smoother skin. This natural remedy can be used topically or taken internally to promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Phenolic acids: They possess antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause and exacerbate pimples.
Anthraquinone glycosides (aloin and aloe-emodin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and clear the skin of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe barbadensis for tinctures is its leaves, which are rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that help in reducing pimple inflammation and preventing infection.

Gel (from Leaves): The clear gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is used to make tinctures, as it is rich in aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help in treating pimples.

Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): Aloe vera extract from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is commonly used in tinctures for pimples, due to its ability to reduce redness, inflammation, and bacterial growth, promoting faster healing of acne and pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis leaves and trim them into small pieces weighing approximately 1 kg.
Combine the aloe barbadensis pieces with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the herbal tincture to a dark glass bottle and label it, then store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The silymarin compound present in the tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of acne-causing bacteria. By regulating the skin's natural oil production and preventing the blockage of pores, Silybum marianum tinctures can help clear up pimples and promote a smoother, healthier complexion. Its antimicrobial properties also help prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria, resulting in clearer skin.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Flavonoids: They exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help control bacterial growth and prevent breakouts.
Silicic acid: It has astringent properties, which can help reduce sebum production and tighten pores to prevent clogged pores and pimples.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather the ingredients - 2 cups of silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of 95% ethanol.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with the ethanol in a glass jar and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks allowing the flavors to mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's ability to combat bacterial and fungal infections can reduce the severity and occurrence of acne. Additionally, its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples. By regulating the body's natural response to inflammation, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may help to promote healthy skin and prevent future breakouts. This natural remedy can provide a holistic approach to acne treatment.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples, such as acne caused by Propionibacterium acnes.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to pimple formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that may cause pimples.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction purposes.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container to remove solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with pimples because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause acne. These properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and promoting healing, leading to a decrease in the occurrence and severity of pimples. Additionally, it may also help to regulate hormones that can contribute to acne.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agent, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.

Flavonoids (including rutin and hyperoside): Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, thereby reducing the occurrence of pimples.
Naphthodianthrones (including hypericin): Shows photodynamic activity, which can help to kill bacteria that cause acne and reduce inflammation in the skin, thus helping to prevent and treat pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and fight bacteria that cause pimples.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to combat acne-causing bacteria.
Stems: Contain a combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them useful for acne treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers of hypericum perforatum when in full bloom for best potency and flavor.
Clean and dry the collected plant material in a warm place protected from direct sunlight for several days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by volume.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tinctures helps with pimples because of its unique properties and compounds. The tincture's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects work together to reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples. Saponaria officinalis contains saponins, which have been shown to have a cleansing effect on the skin, helping to remove dirt and bacteria that can clog pores and cause breakouts. This natural remedy can also help to balance the skin's pH, leading to a reduction in pimples and a clearer complexion.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with pimples by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of skin lesions, thereby preventing further damage and acne progression.
Tannins: Assist with pimples by exhibiting antimicrobial and astringent properties, which help to reduce bacterial growth and tighten pores, resulting in a decrease in acne severity.

Flavonoids: Contribute to pimple treatment by acting as antioxidants, which neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and inflammation, ultimately reducing the appearance and severity of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in pimples.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their astringent properties, which help to dry out pimples and reduce their appearance.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and heal pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 20g of dried plant material into a fine powder.
Combine 200ml of 80% ethanol with 20g of dried plant material in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days then strain and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tannins present in the plant help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its astringent properties prevent bacteria from growing and causing infection. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy and balanced skin environment, which in turn reduces the occurrence of pimples and acne. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: These astringent compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can cause pimples.
Hamamelitannin: This specific tannin in Hamamelis virginiana has antimicrobial properties, which help control the spread of bacteria that cause acne and pimples.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce acne and promote skin healing.

Buds: The buds are used to make tinctures because they contain a high concentration of hamamelin, a natural compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pimple symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections, making them effective in treating acne and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned plant material with 500 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The tincture contains flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples, such as Staphylococcus aureus. Additionally, Taraxacum officinale has been found to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a clear and healthy complexion. This results in a reduction in the occurrence and severity of pimples.
remedy_constituents: Apolyphenols: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with acne and pimples.
Taraxasterol: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of pimples by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause acne.

Taraxasterol acetate: This triterpene ester has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, similar to taraxasterol, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm pimple-prone skin.
Roots: They are used for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional gentle shaking.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the container.
